zh.lifeSummary: 當Marie MARTIN於大約1670年出生在法國阿爾薩斯巴-爾普萊時，她的父親Nicolas Martin24歲，母親Odile HANZO27歲。她於1695年與Dominique GRANDADAM在法國阿爾薩斯巴-爾可爾洛-拉-洛結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1733年4月2日在法國阿爾薩斯巴-爾斯泰享年64歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Marie MARTIN于大约1670年出生在法国阿爾薩斯巴-爾普萊时，她的父亲Nicolas Martin24岁，母亲Odile HANZO27岁。她于1695年与Dominique GRANDADAM在法国阿爾薩斯巴-爾可爾洛-拉-洛结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1733年4月2日在法国阿爾薩斯巴-爾斯泰享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: MARTIN
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
English: variant of   Marton  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

variants.name: Marczewski
meanings: Polish: habitational name for someone from Marcewo (formerly also Marczewo) in Greater Poland Voivodeship. Alternatively, a derivative of the personal name Marc, Marcz, a pet form of Marcin, Marek (see Martin , Mark ).

variants.name: Marciniak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Eugeniusz, Ewa, Agnieszka, Bartosz, Boleslaw, Ignatius, Jaroslaw, Jerzy, Krystyna, Ryszard, Slawomir, Stanislawa.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marak
meanings: Czech:
(Marák): from a pet form of the personal names Martin or Marek .
(Mařák): metronymic from the female personal name Máří, Czech form of Maria . It may also denoted the husband of a woman named Máří.

variants.name: Machain
meanings: Basque: Castilianized form of Matxain, a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with a diminutive of the personal name Martin .

variants.name: Maertens
meanings: Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from the personal name Maerten, a variant of Martin .
North German (Märtens): see Martens .

variants.name: Marcin
meanings: Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia), and Polish: from Marcin, a personal name equivalent to Martin . In Poland this is the most usual form as a personal name, but the surname is very rare there, while in Slovakia, where Marcin is a dialect form of the name Martin, the situation is opposite.
American shortened form of Polish Marcinkiewicz and other derivatives of the personal name Marcin (see 1 above).
In some cases possibly also French: from a vernacular form of the personal name Marcien, derived from the Latin surname Marcianus, popularized through the cult of a local saint.

variants.name: Macho
meanings: Spanish: nickname from macho ‘male; mule’ (from Latin masculus ‘male, masculine’), applied either to denote a virile, strong man or alternatively an idiot.
Spanish: possibly also a nickname for a forceful person or a metonymic occupational name for a smith, from macho ‘sledgehammer’ (from Latin marculus ‘hammer’).
Slovak: from a derivative of the personal name   Martin  .

variants.name: Marcinowski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Aleksander, Bogdan, Jerzy, Zbigniew, Zigmund.
Polish: habitational name for someone from any of the places called Marcinów, Marcinowa, Marcinowice, or any of numerous other places in Poland named with the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marcinkiewicz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Henryk, Beata, Elzbieta, Janina.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcinek, a petform of Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Mar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Gim, Wai, Fong, Hong, Man Chung, Moo, Mun, Quon, Wing, Yat, Bok, Chun.
Spanish, Portuguese, Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Catalan: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mar ‘sea’ (from Latin mare).
German: nickname from Middle High German mar(w) ‘tender, delicate’, or an altered form of Mahr, a topographic name from mar ‘lake, swamp, stagnant water’.

nameMeanings.name: Marie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Maria . When first introduced to England in the Middle Ages, it was Anglicized in pronunciation and respelled Mary . This French form was reintroduced into the English-speaking world as a separate name in the 19th century, and is still pronounced more or less in the French manner, although sometimes with the stress on the first syllable. It is now often used in combination with other names such as Ellie, Chloe, and Lisa.

historicEvents.title: The Palace of Versailles
historicEvents.description: The Palace of Versailles was originally known as a hunting log and retreat for Louis XII. When Louis XIV took over he moved his family into it. After he died the palace was abandoned. It has since been turned into a museum.

historicEvents.title: French Monarchy
historicEvents.description: The French monarchy started way before the 1700s, but for the most part the major events of history took place during this time before the French Revolution started. This was known as the Bourbon Dynasty. Louis XIV the Great, Louis XV the Beloved, Louis XVI the Restorer of French Liberty, and Louis XVII all reigned during this time.

en.lifeSummary: When Marie MARTIN was born about 1670, in Plaine, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, France, her father, Nicolas Martin, was 24 and her mother, Odile HANZO, was 27. She married Dominique GRANDADAM in 1695, in Colroy-la-Roche, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died on 2 April 1733, in Steige, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, France, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Marie MARTIN est née environ 1670, à Plaine, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, France, son père, Nicolas Martin, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Odile HANZO, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Dominique GRANDADAM en 1695, à Colroy-la-Roche, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 2 avril 1733, à Steige, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, France, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.Colroy-la-Roche, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, Franc: Colroy la Roche, Niederrhein, Elsaß, Frankreich
de.lifeSummary: Als Marie MARTIN um 1670 in Blen, Niederrhein, Elsaß, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Nicolas Martin 24 und ihre Mutter Odile HANZO 27. Sie hat 1695 in Colroy la Roche, Niederrhein, Elsaß, Frankreich Dominique GRANDADAM geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 2. April 1733 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Steige, Niederrhein, Elsaß, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Marie MARTIN è nata il circa 1670 nel Plaine, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, Francia, sua padre, Nicolas Martin, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Odile HANZO, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Dominique GRANDADAM nel 1695, nel Colroy-la-Roche, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì il 2 aprile 1733, nel Steige, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, Francia, all'età di 64 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Marie MARTINが約1670年にフランスアルサスバス-ルヒンプレアインで生まれた時，父Nicolas Martinは24歳，母Odile HANZOは27歳であった。1695年にフランスアルサスバス-ルヒンコルロイ-ラ-ロチェでDominique GRANDADAMと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1733年4月2日にフランスアルサスバス-ルヒンステイッジで64歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Marie MARTIN(이)가 대략 1670년 프랑스 알사스 바스-히 플레이인에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicolas Martin(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Odile HANZO(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1695년 프랑스 알사스 바스-히 코로이-라-록헤에서 Dominique GRANDADAM(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1733년 4월 2일 프랑스 알사스 바스-히 스테익에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Marie MARTIN nasceu aproximadamente 1670, em Plaine, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, França, seu pai, Nicolas Martin, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Odile HANZO, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Dominique GRANDADAM em 1695, em Colroy-la-Roche, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 2 de abril de 1733, em Steige, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, França, com 64 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Marie MARTIN родилась около 1670 г. в местности плеин, бас-рин, алсеис, Франция, её отцу по имени Nicolas Martin было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Odile HANZO, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Dominique GRANDADAM в 1695 г. в местности колрои-ла-рош, бас-рин, алсеис, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла 2 апреля 1733 г. в местности стидж, бас-рин, алсеис, Франция в возрасте 64 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Marie MARTIN nació aproximadamente 1670, en Plaine, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, Francia, su papá, Nicolas Martin, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Odile HANZO, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Dominique GRANDADAM en 1695, en Colroy-la-Roche, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 2 de abril de 1733, en Steige, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, Francia, a la edad de 64 años.
